original_hypothesis.title: Public airdrop config setter enables mid-claim window griefing or permanent claim lockout (misconfiguration or malicious owner action)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: MerkleClaimable.setConfig() allows the owner to change claimStart/claimEnd/merkleRoot at any time with no sanity checks (e.g., claimStart < claimEnd, nonzero window). If invoked during an active claim period, it can immediately halt claims (merkleRoot=0 or claimStart>now or claimEnd<now), or switch to a new root unexpectedly, invalidating operational assumptions and potentially causing large-scale claim failures.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is operationally realistic because the deployment script initially sets (0,0,0) and expects later configuration. Any mistake or compromised governance path can freeze claims during critical windows. Validate whether production governance has safeguards (timelock/roles/procedures) preventing disruptive mid-claim changes.
deployment_assumptions: Owner key/governance can call setConfig during or near active claim window
No off-chain policy or timelock prevents emergency or accidental changes
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployERC20Airdrop.s.sol initializes ERC20Airdrop with claimStart=0, claimEnd=0, merkleRoot=0, implying post-deploy setConfig is required; no on-chain constraints exist in MerkleClaimable._setConfig.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setConfig() -> _setConfig() assigns claimStart/claimEnd/merkleRoot with no validation; ongoingClaim requires nonzero + time bounds

preconditions: Caller has owner privileges on the airdrop contract
Claims are expected to be live (users submitting claim txs)
Owner can update config without an on-chain timelock on this contract

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Contract is deployable with claimStart=0/claimEnd=0/merkleRoot=0 then later configured; any incorrect config instantly blocks all claims via ongoingClaim modifier.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Single owner call controls claim availability and eligibility set.

operational_questions: Is setConfig protected by protocol governance timelock in production deployments (or is it a hot owner key)?
Are there runbooks preventing config changes during active claim windows?
Do monitoring/alerts exist for merkleRoot/claim window changes?
validation_requirements: Review on-chain owner for each airdrop instance and its governance controls
Simulate setConfig changes mid-claim and measure user-facing impact
Confirm whether any other contract depends on fixed claim window parameters

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify active airdrop instance and confirm owner is an EOA or governance module
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Owner (or compromised governance) calls setConfig with merkleRoot=0 or claimEnd < now
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: All subsequent claims revert with CLAIM_NOT_ONGOING
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Optionally rotate merkleRoot to a modified tree to redirect eligibility (requires further governance compromise)

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Control over eligibility and claim liveness for all users of that instance
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Mass claim failures, support load, reputational damage; potential permanent lockout if config not corrected
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium—recoverable by setting correct parameters, but only if governance can act promptly
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All airdrop claim flows in derived contracts

execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: Low if owner is a hot key; higher if timelocked governance
execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Most damaging during peak claim window

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Events/txs calling setConfig; abrupt merkleRoot changes
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Spike in reverted claims with CLAIM_NOT_ONGOING
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Config updates shortly before claimStart or during claims

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
